meaning: n.楔,木楔,楔形物vt.用楔子垫,收放定盘上adv.满满地
examples: The shelves were chock-a-block with ornaments.
The small roads are chock-a-block with traffic.
The 32-page catalogue is chock-full of things that add fun to festive occasions.
Gaming blogs and Web sites are chock-full of complaints alleging that the 360 freezes, overheats and crashes.
Other western New York state and western Pennsylvania metros are also chock-full of bars.
